product: Confluent REST Proxy (Confluent Platform, current docs) — v2 consumer API

media_types.records_accept: application/vnd.kafka.json.v2+json — Accept for GET records when the consumer was created with format=json; the embedded format in the Accept header must match the consumer's format or the proxy answers 406/40601

body.name: instance name, used in consumer URLs
body.auto.commit.enable: false — offsets are committed explicitly, only after processing

response.base_uri: URI of the specific proxy instance holding this consumer; subsequent requests MUST go to it

errors.409/40902: Consumer instance with the specified name already exists

fetch.response: array of {topic, key, value, partition, offset}

errors.406/40601: consumer format does not match the embedded format in Accept

commit.rules: commit the offset of the last processed record per (topic, partition), entries sorted by topic then partition; committed only AFTER the batch handler succeeded; an empty POST body would commit everything fetched so far, which this client never uses

delete.rule: always issued exactly once when the instance was created — on success, handler error, proxy error, and cancellation; never when create itself failed

session_rules.instance_affinity: requests after create use the returned base_uri, but only when its scheme://host:port equals the configured proxy origin; otherwise the session aborts with failure UNTRUSTED_BASE_URI, sends nothing (especially no credentials) to the foreign origin, and cleans up via the configured origin path /consumers/{group}/instances/{instance_id}
session_rules.redirects: the transport never follows HTTP redirects; a 3xx on the records fetch aborts the session with failure REDIRECT_BLOCKED and still deletes the instance
session_rules.empty_batch: a fetch returning [] ends the session with outcome COMPLETED

auth.header: Authorization on every request to the trusted origin
